title: Saint Lubin (Leobinus)
description: Bishop of Chartres, venerated in Catholic tradition. Origin of the name's usage in France as a masculine first name. The name derives from the Germanic baptismal name Leobin, meaning 'dear' or 'beloved'.

title: Lubin Baugin
description: French Baroque painter known for religious works and still lifes. His famous painting 'Nature morte à l'échiquier' (Still Life with Chessboard) is displayed at the Musée du Louvre. He was Painter to the King in 1657.

title: "Annette et Lubin"
description: Major literary work by Jean-François Marmontel featuring the name Lubin as the male protagonist. This pastoral tale was adapted into operas by multiple composers and performed throughout Europe.

title: "Au clair de la lune"
description: Traditional French folk song featuring a character named Lubin. The song has been sung throughout France for centuries and is taught in schools.

title: Lubin de Beauvais
description: French painter, watercolourist, and illustrator. He collaborated with major Parisian newspapers and magazines including Le Rire and La Vie Parisienne. His works are held in the Musée d'Orsay collection.

title: Early 20th Century Usage
description: Between 1900 and 2009, approximately 1,630 boys were named Lubin in France, showing continuous usage throughout the modern era.

title: Contemporary Peak
description: Over 2,530 boys named Lubin in France during this period, with a peak of 245 births in 2012. As of 2024, Lubin ranked 623rd among boys' names with 70 births.
